title: UMI Image Layered World Model
description: UMI Image Layered World Model: three linked papers covering multi-view robot video world modeling, manipulation-video dynamics decomposition into reusable layers, and layered data as a downstream VLA/IDM/reward training interface.
summary: UMI is split into three publishable stages: Stage 1 a streaming multi-view pose/action-conditioned UMI World Model for wrist/head/fixed views; Stage 2 a Video Dynamics Decomposition Model (VDDM) that decomposes robot and egocentric human-hand manipulation videos into actor/object-contact/occluder/scene layers plus geometry/tracking/state sidecars; Stage 3 a layered-robot-data VLA/video-model training paradigm that tests IDM, reward, pose/flow, augmentation, and PhysisForcing-style physics alignment beyond raw RGB or mask-only baselines. Dataset discovery for Stage 2/3 starts from Datasets Bot (https://datasets.bot/) and still requires license, schema, overlap, and leakage audits before any training use.

subprojects.label: A
subprojects.title: Stage 1: Streaming Multi-View UMI World Model
subprojects.body: Paper 1 story: learn a streaming pose/action-conditioned UMI world model from left/right wrist plus head/fixed views, but the next gate is falsifiable closed-loop validation before another expensive WAN/Gamma/shared-world scale-up. Data split must mix DaiMeng UMI-style data, AGI-World, and previous JianZhi/UMI-like data while separating train/val/test by task, object, scene, embodiment, and camera setup. Baselines must include copy-last-frame / first-frame hold, nearest-neighbor retrieval, wrist-only or D435-only prediction, current best shared-world teacher/ranker, action-swapped counterfactuals, and generic interactive world models fed UMI first frames as negative baselines. Win metrics: same-episode rollout fidelity, cross-view consistency, object/contact state prediction, actual-vs-zero/wrong/nearest action separation, planner/reward/IDM utility, and cross-split generalization. The UMI cross-embodiment hypothesis should be tested separately: most visual gap is jaw/end-effector appearance, and wrist-view jaw position is relatively fixed.
subprojects.output: closed-loop eval protocol + DaiMeng/AGI-World/JianZhi split + multi-view rollout + action/counterfactual gates + embodiment-gap ablation

subprojects.label: B
subprojects.title: Stage 2: Video Dynamics Decomposition Model
subprojects.body: Paper 2 story: train/evaluate a Video Dynamics Decomposition Model (VDDM) for manipulation videos, not just a mask/inpaint script. The minimum probe should cover 3 robot clips plus 3 egocentric human-hand clips, all exported as actor_layer(actor_type=robot|human_hand), object_contact_layer, occluder_tool_layer, and scene_background_layer. Sidecars must include mask.png, RGBA, layer video, SVG contour, depth/pointmap, mesh/pose if available, 2D/3D tracks, contact events, object/robot state estimates, and layer_manifest.json. Robot route: RobotSeg/SAM3/SAM2 tracking plus VACE, FLUX.fill, and LaMa baselines. Human-ego route: HaWoR and Do-As-I-Do-style stacks, roughly SAM3 -> SAM3D mesh -> MoGe pointmaps -> HaWoR -> TAPIR/BootsTAPIR or guided-diffusion tracking -> projection/retargeting. Evaluation compares raw RGB, mask-only, and VDDM layers for recomposition, temporal consistency, track/pose/reconstruction/retargeting, and failure modes.
subprojects.output: 3 robot + 3 ego-human layer packages + actor/object-contact/occluder/scene schema + state/geometry/tracking sidecars + reconstruction/retargeting QA

subprojects.label: C
subprojects.title: Stage 3: Layered Data Utility for VLA
subprojects.body: Paper 3 story: prove that layered robot data changes downstream training, not just visualization. Scene layer should feed RoboEngine-style augmentation plus MoGe/GeoCalib geometry and calibration. Object/contact layer should feed SAM3/SAM3D/Fast-SAM3D/FoundationPose/Fast-FoundationStereoPhysics/Orient-Anything/Any6D for pose and shape, plus BootsTAPIR/TAPNet/AllTracker/D4RT/CoTracker/Any4D with Video Depth Anything or MoGe for 3D point flow. Robot/hand layer should feed Dr. Robot, ORB-SLAM3/VGGT-SLAM, HaWoR, and dex-retargeting. Add a PhysisForcing-style video-model training probe: convert VDDM layer outputs into confidence-gated physics_forcing sidecars, then train the same video backbone with pixel-level trajectory alignment and semantic inter-region relation alignment on physics-informative robot/object/contact regions. Compare raw RGB, mask-only, object-only, object+background, robot-only, recomposed clips, raw tracker/depth forcing, and layer+state forcing; the target is physically plausible object motion, contact coupling, matched object outcome, contact intent, grasp/tool-use mode, and task result, not joint-angle imitation.
subprojects.output: scene/object/robot utility ablations + physics_forcing sidecar schema + PhysisForcing-style same-backbone training ablation + v0 pipeline/tool selection

asset_alt: Shared-Backbone Dual-Branch Wan architecture diagram for UMI, showing left and right gripper camera histories, shared video VAE/tokenizer, condition encoder, view embeddings, shared Wan backbone, dual prediction heads, and training objectives.
asset_caption: Shared-Backbone Dual-Branch Wan: UMI Stage 1 architecture for separate left/right wrist video latents, view embeddings, shared Wan backbone, dual decoded outputs, and cross-view / temporal / state-action consistency losses.

layer_utility.title: Layer utility map
layer_utility.aria_label: Image-layered robot data utility map linking scene, object, and robot layers to downstream model families and third-party visual processing tools.
layer_utility.caption: Layer order follows recomposition: robot/object layers overlay upward onto the scene layer. Stage 3 treats each layer as an interface to external vision/geometry/retargeting tools, then checks whether the resulting signals improve IDM, reward, pose/flow, augmentation, and PhysisForcing-style video-model physics alignment beyond raw RGB and mask-only baselines.

layers.name: Scene / Background
layers.summary: Use the scene/background layer as stable physical context: background replacement, camera/depth/gravity estimation, and task-aware augmentation.

groups.label: Depth / camera / gravity calibration

repositories.label: MoGe
repositories.note: Monocular geometry for metric point maps, depth, normals, and camera FOV.

groups.note: Scene layer should provide the metric/geometric frame for object 3D pose, point flow lifting, and wrist/head-view alignment; if MoGe/GeoCalib disagree with real calibration, use them only as priors.

layers.name: Object(s) / Contact
layers.summary: Use object/contact layers for masks, 3D shape, 6-DoF/object pose, point flow, hand-object consistency, process reward evidence, and physics-forcing supervision for video world models.

groups.label: 2D mask / region tracking

repositories.label: SAM3
repositories.note: Promptable image/video segmentation and tracking for task-relevant objects.

groups.note: Keep SAM3 here as a Stage 3 measurement input as well as a Stage 2 mask source: object IDs, contact patch masks, and failure cases should be checked against layer recomposition.

groups.label: 3D shape + 6-DoF / pose

repositories.label: SAM 3D Objects
repositories.note: Single-image masked object -> 3D model with pose, shape, texture, and layout.

repositories.label: Fast-SAM3D
repositories.note: Acceleration route for SAM3D-style single-view 3D reconstruction.

groups.note: Use FoundationPose-family methods when a CAD/template or RGB-D setup is available; use SAM 3D Objects/Fast-SAM3D as a single-image shape/layout probe for missing object geometry.

groups.label: Object point flow / motion

repositories.label: BootsTAPIR / TAPNet
repositories.note: TAPIR/BootsTAPIR point tracking for object surface motion and contact-adjacent trajectories.

groups.note: For cloth/fluid/deformable objects, report 2D tracks and lifted 3D point-cloud flow separately; depth/calibration uncertainty should be part of QA.

groups.label: HOI reconstruction / retargeting bridge

repositories.label: Do-As-I-Do
repositories.note: Human RGB video -> hand-object reconstruction -> robot retargeting reference.

groups.note: Use Do-As-I-Do as the Stage 3 bridge between object/contact evidence and robot action: matched object pose/velocity/contact intent is the target, not human-vs-robot joint-angle imitation.

groups.label: Video-model physics forcing

repositories.label: PhysisForcing project
repositories.note: Region-focused hierarchical physics alignment for manipulation video generation.

groups.note: Map VDDM sidecars to PhysisForcing-style training signals: physics-informative masks, point trajectories, object/robot state, contact patches, and inter-layer relation targets. Treat as a same-backbone ablation against whole-frame finetuning, mask-only weighting, and raw tracker/depth forcing.

layers.name: Robot(s) / Hand
layers.summary: Use robot/hand layers for robot pose, wrist/head ego-motion, human-hand source motion, HOI retargeting, and IDM supervision.

groups.label: Human hand tracking / retargeting source

repositories.label: HaWoR
repositories.note: World-space hand motion reconstruction from egocentric videos.

groups.note: Use this when the source is human video or no visible robot layer exists; the validation target is object-state/contact-consistent retargeting into Franka/Wuji or other robot hands.

details: Claim: Image-Layered Robot Data should improve manipulation only if it helps downstream robot learning, not merely because the decomposed images look clean.

Stage 1 acceptance: generated robot-camera videos must share overlapping scene, object, contact, and action consequences instead of drifting as independent clips. Mainline is shared-world / Gamma-style multi-view teacher first; WAN2.2 action-control remains a controlled adapter ablation until it passes strict actual/zero/wrong-row gates.

Stage 1 validation update 2026-06-28: Gaochen discussion reframes the near-term blocker as closed-loop validation cost, not only architecture. Before scaling another expensive route, define where to compare, which baseline is fair, and how DaiMeng + AGI-World / previous JianZhi-selected data are split by task/object/scene/embodiment. Generic interactive world models collapsing on UMI first frames is a useful negative baseline but not enough; include copy-last-frame, nearest-neighbor retrieval, wrist-only/current shared-world teacher, and action-swapped counterfactual baselines.

Stage 2 acceptance: every layer batch must preserve raster truth and structure: masks, RGBA/video, inpainted background, optional SVG/contact metadata, layer_manifest.json, temporal consistency, and recomposition QA.
Stage 3 acceptance: compare flat RGB, mask-only/SAM, and full layered data on the same tasks before claiming gains in IDM, FoundationPose, reward/eval, background replacement, or policy training.

Current open execution gates: WAN2.2 action encoder / injection redesign, P3 RobotSeg-to-SAM3 segmentation adapter with gripper-first part priors, VACE/Gemini-Omni inpainting QA with LaMa as a negative baseline, and layer utility testing for object pose/flow, robot pose/VO, scene augmentation, and PhysisForcing-style state supervision.

Dataset substrate: Hoi! is the selected tri-view / multi ego-view replacement candidate because it provides synchronized head-ego, manipulator-centric ego, and fixed external views; MV-UMI and EgoMimic remain references/backups.

Dataset discovery update 2026-06-28: use Datasets Bot (https://datasets.bot/) as a Stage 2/3 real-robot dataset source surface. Use it to discover physical manipulation datasets for VDDM decomposition and layered-data utility ablations, but treat every entry as a lead that still needs license, modality, camera/action schema, train/test leakage, and task-overlap audit before inclusion.

Three-paper split 2026-06-27: Stage 1 is the streaming multi-view pose/action-conditioned UMI World Model; Stage 2 is a Video Dynamics Decomposition Model (VDDM) that turns robot and egocentric human-hand manipulation videos into reusable dynamic layers; Stage 3 is the downstream VLA/IDM/reward paper proving layered data changes learning and evaluation, not only visual quality.

Stage 2 VDDM boundary 2026-06-27: treat segmentation, mask tracking, VACE/FLUX/LaMa/GPT inpainting, SVG contour sidecars, and geometry/track extraction as modules inside a model/data interface. The claim is temporally consistent manipulation dynamics decomposition with actor/object-contact/occluder/scene semantics; it should accept robot data and egocentric human-hand manipulation videos via actor_type=robot|human_hand.

Stage 2 reconstruction bridge 2026-06-27: use HaWoR and Do-As-I-Do-style reconstruction as downstream probes. A useful VDDM layer output should make SAM3/SAM3D mesh, MoGe pointmaps, HaWoR hand motion, TAPIR/BootsTAPIR or guided-diffusion tracking, projection, and retargeting cleaner than raw RGB or mask-only inputs.

Stage 3 VLA utility boundary 2026-06-27: the paper should measure whether scene/object-contact/robot-hand layers improve policy learning signals: object pose/velocity/contact outcome, IDM action inference, reward/progress models, background augmentation, and HOI-consistent retargeting.

PhysisForcing integration 2026-06-30: use VDDM outputs as physics-informative supervision for video world models, not as another cosmetic layer label. Export a physics_forcing sidecar with scene mask/depth/camera priors, robot mask/state/keypoints, object/contact mask/pose/point-flow/contact events, and inter-layer relation targets such as gripper-object coupling, pushed-object motion, and contact onset. Training ablation: same backbone with (1) whole-frame finetune, (2) mask-only weighted loss, (3) raw tracker/depth forcing without layers, and (4) VDDM layer+state forcing. Success requires better physical plausibility and action/object outcome prediction under the same rollout/test split; noisy pose or flow estimates must be confidence-gated rather than forced blindly.

Dataset download audit 2026-07-04: remote queue state is now the source of truth at /mnt/data/datasets/dual_wrist_serial_queue/. Keep genrobot2025/10Kh-RealOmin-OpenData as the sole active external downloader; queue XRZero first after it, then Mobile ALOHA, LeRobot Shirt-Folding, ManipArena, MolmoAct2 Bimanual YAM, Galaxea, AgiBotWorld2026, ABC-130k, and RoboTwin2.0. Do not start these in parallel with 10Kh unless the single-download policy is explicitly changed.

Dataset format bridge 2026-07-04: the remote Gamma/UMI loader expects robot189-style paired streams under video/{left,right}, caption/{left,right}, and action/{left,right}; actions are 10-D pos3 + rot6d + gripper arrays. New datasets must be converted into that contract before being treated as Stage 1/2 training substrate.

Serial scheduler 2026-07-04: /mnt/data/datasets/dual_wrist_serial_queue/serial_dataset_scheduler.py is armed in waiting mode. It only polls 10Kh completion; while 10Kh is active it leaves XRZero scheduler_hold in place. After 10Kh is complete and no non-10Kh process is active, it starts only XRZero with WORKERS=2. Mobile ALOHA, LeRobot Folding, ManipArena, Galaxea, AgiBot, ABC, RoboTwin, and local materialization remain manual/adapter-gated.

references.title: Gamma-World: Generative Multi-Agent World Modeling Beyond Two Players
references.notes: MAIN REFERENCE / Stage 1 build-upon route. Gamma-World defines the synchronized action-conditioned interactive world-model lane, with Simplex Rotary Agent Encoding, Sparse Hub Attention, and causal real-time rollout. Use it as the Stage 1 reference for UMI Ego World generation and shared-context multi-view rollout; then make the UMI contribution about robot-data utility, view/layer/eval contracts, IDM/control decoding, and policy ablations rather than agent-count scaling.

references.title: COMBO: Compositional World Models for Embodied Multi-Agent Cooperation
references.notes: Stage 1 shared-context reference. COMBO estimates overall world state from partial egocentric RGBD observations, factorizes multi-agent joint actions into compositional action conditions, and uses the resulting world model with VLM-assisted action proposal / intent tracking / outcome evaluation for online cooperation. Use it to frame UMI left/right/head views as partial observations of one latent scene, not independent video targets.

references.title: Solaris: Building a Multiplayer Video World Model in Minecraft
references.notes: Stage 1 multi-view consistency reference. Solaris builds a synchronized multiplayer video+action data system, trains through staged single-player to multiplayer modeling with causal and Self Forcing variants, introduces Checkpointed Self Forcing for longer-horizon supervision, and evaluates movement, memory, grounding, building, and view consistency. Use it as the strongest analogy for UMI's multiple generated camera streams sharing the same world state.

references.title: Dashboard asset: world-model taxonomy
references.notes: User-provided taxonomy figure. Keep as a visual guardrail for Tri-View: reconstruction models, next-step predictive models, and runnable simulators are different world-model claims and need different evaluation evidence.

references.title: Qwen-Image-Layered: Layered Decomposition for Inherent Editability
references.notes: NEGATIVE BASELINE / passed over for current robot-layer pipeline. Group tests on 2026-06-17/18 showed Qwen-Image-Layered can control layer count but cannot bind required robot/object/background semantics to each layer; prompt constraints act like captions, producing merged entities, residual/shadow/support layers, failed pure-background rendering, and weak inpainting. Keep as evidence that generic editable layers are insufficient for robot manipulation.

references.title: RevealLayer: Disentangling Hidden and Visible Layers via Occlusion-Aware Image Decomposition
references.notes: REFERENCE ONLY / not the short-term practical route after group testing. RevealLayer's occlusion-aware RGBA formulation remains useful background, but the current robot-scene probe was judged unusable. Do not depend on RevealLayer as the Stage 2 mainline unless new robot-specific guidance or code evidence changes this result.

references.title: ObjectForesight: Predicting Future 3D Object Trajectories from Human Videos
references.notes: MAIN REFERENCE / object-centric dynamics route. ObjectForesight predicts future 6-DoF object trajectories from short egocentric video histories and builds large-scale pseudo-ground-truth object trajectory data from human videos using segmentation, tracking, reconstruction, depth/camera motion, and pose estimation. Use as the reference for object/contact layer targets and for the segment+track+reconstruct data pipeline.

references.title: Walk through Paintings: Egocentric World Models from Internet Priors
references.notes: Support reference for Ego World: transforms pretrained video diffusion models into action-conditioned egocentric world models with motor-command conditioning and structural consistency evaluation.

references.title: LOME: Learning Human-Object Manipulation with Action-Conditioned Egocentric World Model
references.notes: Support reference for action-conditioned egocentric human-object interaction video: uses reference image, text prompt, and per-frame actions to generate physically plausible manipulation videos.

references.title: EgoSim: Egocentric World Simulator for Embodied Interaction Generation
references.notes: Support reference for continuously updated egocentric interaction simulation with 3D state updating, geometry-action-aware observation simulation, and scalable egocentric video data construction.

references.title: EVA: Aligning Video World Models with Executable Robot Actions via Inverse Dynamics Rewards
references.notes: Support reference for IDM: generated video rollouts can be decoded into actions by an inverse dynamics model, and IDM-derived rewards can align video generation with executable robot constraints.

references.title: PhysisForcing: Physics Reinforced World Simulator for Robotic Manipulation
references.notes: Stage 3 video-model training reference. PhysisForcing focuses supervision on physics-informative regions using pixel-level point-trajectory alignment and semantic inter-region relation alignment. For UMI, test whether VDDM layer packages provide cleaner forcing signals: scene geometry, object state, robot state, contact patches, point trajectories, and actor-object-scene relations. Treat it as an ablation candidate; do not claim layer data helps until same-backbone whole-frame, mask-only, raw-tracker, and VDDM layer+state comparisons pass.

references.title: RoboEngine: Plug-and-Play Robot Data Augmentation
references.notes: Background-layer / scene-adaptation reference. RoboEngine uses semantic robot segmentation and background generation for physics- and task-aware robot data augmentation, matching the scene-layer domain-randomization track.

references.title: Single-View Robot Pose and Joint Angle Estimation via Render & Compare
references.notes: Support reference for visible-robot branch: RoboPose estimates robot joint angles and 6D camera-to-robot pose from a single RGB image of a known articulated robot.

references.title: ORB-SLAM3: An Accurate Open-Source Library for Visual, Visual-Inertial and Multi-Map SLAM
references.notes: Support reference for no-arm-visible branch: visual/visual-inertial SLAM supports monocular, stereo, RGB-D, pinhole, and fisheye cameras, suitable as a VO/SLAM baseline for wrist/head ego views.

references.title: VGGT: Visual Geometry Grounded Transformer
references.notes: Support reference for feed-forward visual geometry: predicts camera parameters, depth, point maps, and 3D point tracks from one or many views, useful for wrist/head egomotion and object-scene geometry.

references.title: SANA-WM: Efficient Minute-Scale World Modeling with Hybrid Linear Diffusion Transformer
references.notes: Reference for efficient minute-scale world modeling, hybrid linear attention, precise 6-DoF camera control, and metric pose-supervised long-video generation.

references.title: iWorld-Bench: A Benchmark for Interactive World Models with a Unified Action Generation Framework
references.notes: Reference for evaluating interactive/camera-controllable world models with packaged metadata, camera trajectories, action-control, memory-ability, camera-following, and all-metric evaluation wrappers.

references.title: EgoX: Egocentric Video Generation from a Single Exocentric Video
references.notes: Exo-to-ego reference: point-cloud rendered ego prior, width/channel latent concatenation, geometry-guided self-attention, LoRA on Wan 2.1 14B; useful for cross-view conditioning design.

references.title: Intention-driven Ego-to-Exo Video Generation
references.notes: Ego-to-exo reference: uses view-independent intention, head trajectory, action description, optical flow and occlusion maps to synthesize exocentric video from egocentric input.

references.title: WorldWander: Bridging Egocentric and Exocentric Worlds in Video Generation
references.notes: Bidirectional ego/exo translation reference with in-context perspective alignment and synchronized ego-exo triplets; relevant for tri-view cross-view synchronization.

references.title: MolmoAct: Action Reasoning Models that can Reason in Space
references.notes: Dataset-design reference for this project, not a direct substitute for our missing view: MolmoAct's real-robot dataset uses two side-mounted views and one wrist camera, converts episodes into side+wrist paired examples, and adds depth/trace/action-reasoning supervision.

references.title: MolmoAct2-BimanualYAM Dataset
references.notes: LeRobot-format schema reference for synchronized bimanual manipulation data: camera streams are observation.images.top / left / right at 360x640 30fps, with 14D bimanual state/action. Useful for dataset packaging and ablations, but it does not resolve the current UMI/Jianzhi missing fixed robot-ego view.

references.title: Hoi!: A Multimodal Dataset for Force-Grounded, Cross-View Articulated Manipulation
references.notes: Selected dataset replacement substrate for UMI Image Layered World Model on 2026-06-17. Public Hoi! bundles cover synchronized head-ego, manipulator-centric ego / UMI-style, wrist, and fixed external views, making it the best available open data surface for shared-context multi ego-view manifests.

references.title: Datasets Bot: open robotics dataset directory
references.notes: Stage 2/3 dataset discovery surface. Datasets Bot indexes open robotics datasets and exposes machine-readable entries with metadata such as source, license, modality, camera count, action space, schema summary, paper/code/download URLs, and usage snippets. Use it to find additional physical robot/manipulation clips for VDDM decomposition, layer QA, and VLA/IDM/reward utility ablations; do not ingest a candidate until license, camera/action schema, dataset overlap, and train/test leakage checks pass.

references.title: Dual-wrist serial download queue audit
references.notes: Current remote audit and queue generated from Datasets Bot, Open-X-Tactile, Hugging Face metadata, and live Aliyun process state. The enforced policy is one external dataset download at a time: 10Kh stays active, while XRZero, Mobile ALOHA, LeRobot Shirt-Folding, ManipArena, MolmoAct2 Bimanual YAM, Galaxea, AgiBotWorld2026, ABC-130k, and RoboTwin2.0 remain queued or held.

references.title: Open-X-Tactile
references.notes: Tactile ecosystem index inspected on 2026-07-04. The static site-data snapshot has 15 datasets and 12 sensors, with bimanual/dual entries such as RDP_Bimanual and Unit_Bimanual. Treat it as a tactile sidecar/source index for Stage 2/3, not as the primary dual-wrist RGB+action download queue.

references.title: Mobile ALOHA dataset
references.notes: High-priority queued candidate from Datasets Bot: physical bimanual mobile manipulation with overhead plus left/right wrist RGB, 14-D state, 16-D action, language instructions, and about 50.9GB TFDS/RLDS size. Queue after XRZero once 10Kh is done or paused by explicit decision.

references.title: LeRobot Shirt-Folding Dataset
references.notes: High-priority queued candidate from Datasets Bot: real bimanual shirt-folding data in LeRobot v3 with base plus two wrist cameras and 16-D joint+gripper state/action. Hugging Face API reports public ungated storage around 485.6GB.

references.title: ManipArena dataset
references.notes: Strong but gated queued candidate: real/sim bimanual and mobile manipulation with overhead plus two wrist cameras, 56-D proprioception, language annotations, and LeRobot v2.1 format. Download requires access check before it can outrank ungated candidates.

references.title: Galaxea Open-World Dataset
references.notes: Large gated queued candidate: dual-arm mobile manipulator data with head/head-right plus left/right wrist cameras and whole-body actions. Keep later in the queue because Hugging Face API reports roughly 13TB used storage.

references.title: ABC-130k
references.notes: Massive gated bimanual candidate with top plus two wrist views and 14-D state/action. Keep behind smaller exact-wrist candidates because Hugging Face API reports roughly 22TB used storage.

references.title: Dr. Robot: Differentiable Robot Rendering
references.notes: Robot-layer / IDM reference. Dr. Robot makes robot appearance differentiable with respect to control parameters, so it is a natural reference for whole-arm pose/joint recovery and for replacing a generic IDM with robot-kinematic visual gradients.

references.title: ORB-SLAM3: Visual, Visual-Inertial and Multi-Map SLAM
references.notes: Wrist/head ego geometry baseline. ORB-SLAM3 supports visual and visual-inertial SLAM with monocular, stereo, RGB-D, pinhole, and fisheye setups, which matches the need to recover wrist-camera motion when robot pixels are incomplete or absent.

references.title: LIV: Language-Image Representations and Rewards for Robotic Control
references.notes: Object-layer reward reference. LIV pretrains control-centric visual-language representations from human video without action labels and can produce language-conditioned rewards on human and robot videos, which maps to object/contact process reward.

references.title: ARRO: Augmented Reality for Robots
references.notes: Policy-utility evidence. ARRO shows that segmentation-driven augmented robot observations can improve Diffusion Policy and generalist robot policies; this supports the stronger ablation that full Image-Layered Robot Data should help manipulation.

references.title: 10Kh RealOmni-Open DataSet
references.notes: New Stage 1 data source from the UMI model-structure update. GenRobot describes 10Kh RealOmni-Open as real-world, diverse-skill embodied data with 1M+ data clips and ongoing expansion; use it for the dual-wrist Wan2.1 baseline only after checking view/camera availability, download access, license, task coverage, and annotation fields.

references.title: Wan2.2 MoE high-noise / low-noise architecture
references.notes: Training-risk reference for UMI: Wan2.2-A14B uses a two-expert denoising MoE, with a high-noise expert for early/layout steps and a low-noise expert for later/detail steps. This improves quality but complicates custom finetuning because UMI adaptation must decide whether and how to train each expert and where to switch by SNR/timestep.

references.title: Wan2.1 LoRA fine-tuning training guide
references.notes: Implementation reference for the first UMI training route: provides a complete Wan2.1 LoRA workflow covering dataset structure, metadata.json, model download, DeepSpeed/FSDP training, validation, and inference testing.

references.title: Finetrainers Wan training support
references.notes: Implementation reference: finetrainers documents LoRA and full-finetune support and lists tested Wan2.1 T2V/I2V/FLF2V checkpoints. Use as a cleaner baseline toolchain candidate before modifying dual-wrist inputs.

references.title: VACE: All-in-One Video Creation and Editing
references.notes: Video inpainting / masked V2V candidate for Stage 2. Official VACE repo describes reference-to-video, video-to-video editing, masked video-to-video editing, and free composition, making it a direct fit for mask-tracking -> video inpaint -> recomposition QA.

references.title: Wan2.1 VACE model release
references.notes: Wan2.1 VACE release reference. Use for inference/code/weights alignment when testing VACE on robot-mask clips; keep Wan2.1 compatibility with the Stage 1 training route.

references.title: RoboTwin 2.0: scalable dual-arm manipulation data generator and benchmark
references.notes: Candidate mask-rich synthetic data source for Stage 2. RoboTwin 2.0 is built for bimanual manipulation data generation and benchmarking, with SAPIEN-based simulation, strong domain randomization, and a 50-task / 731-object benchmark surface. Group note: inspect whether provided grayscale masks expose per-object mask IDs directly usable for layer pseudo-labels.

references.title: RoboTwin 2.0 official repository
references.notes: Implementation/data-audit target for checking mask files, grayscale ID conventions, camera views, object metadata, and whether generated episodes can export robot/object/contact/occluder/background masks.

references.title: MaskWAM: Unifying Mask Prompting and Prediction for World-Action Models
references.notes: Comparison paper for the mask-centric WAM direction. MaskWAM integrates masks as explicit inputs and future predictions via a unified Mixture of Transformers, addressing spatial ambiguity and semantic grounding. For UMI, borrow the annotation loop and future-mask-prediction lesson, but keep the contribution as reusable robot/object-contact/occluder/scene RGBA/video layers with inpaint, SVG/contact sidecars, recomposition QA, and IDM/reward/FoundationPose utility.

references.title: MaskWAM annotation pipeline figure
references.notes: Dashboard copy of the MaskWAM data-processing figure: language annotation and VLM object extraction initialize SAM3 mask sequences, human point prompts correct ambiguous rounds, and accepted masks become labeled episodes. Use as a practical reference for the global layered dataset annotation loop.

references.title: MaskWAM vs UMI memo + v0 dataset plan (private backup)
references.notes: Moved to PRIVATE BorisGuo6/Gamma-World repo (robot-reproduction branch, docs/research/): MaskWAM-vs-UMI comparison memo + global Image-Layered dataset v0 plan (method matrix / IO schema / cost / QA / utility / failures / next-batch). Not published on the public site. Core distinction: MaskWAM=mask-as-policy-supervision; UMI=layers-as-reusable-data-infrastructure.

references.title: Any6D: novel object 6D pose / size estimation
references.notes: Object-layer utility target from the 2026-06-22 sync. Use object-only, object+background, recomposed, raw RGB/RGB-D, and mask-only inputs to test whether Image-Layered data improves novel object 6D pose/size extraction.

references.title: AllTracker
references.notes: Object-layer tracking reference from the Layered Image Policy chat. Use for dense point tracks / optical-flow-style correspondences; combine with DepthAnything-Video or another temporally consistent depth model when lifting object tracks into 3D point-cloud flow.

references.title: D4RT
references.notes: Object-layer 4D reconstruction / 3D point-tracking alternative. Use as a comparison for whether object/contact layers improve point cloud flow, 3D tracks, and scene/camera consistency.

references.title: FLUX.fill / FLUX.1 Fill inpainting candidate
references.notes: Still-image inpainting candidate recommended for Stage 2. Use against GPT image, LaMa, and VACE; output must preserve original canvas size and be reusable for layer overlay/recomposition.

references.title: LaMa image inpainting
references.notes: Local inpainting fallback for Ziyang when ChatGPT image API access is blocked. Useful as a lightweight mask-inpaint baseline for robot/object removal before video-level VACE tests.

references.title: Do-As-I-Do: Learning Robot Actions from Human Videos
references.notes: Stage 2/3 downstream reference for human-video hand-object reconstruction, SAM3/SAM3D/MoGe/HaWoR/TAPIR-style processing, and HOI-consistent retargeting.

references.title: Do-As-I-Do code
references.notes: Public code reference for the human-video-to-robot-retargeting pipeline.

references.title: HaWoR: World-Space Hand Motion Reconstruction from Egocentric Videos
references.notes: Stage 2/3 egocentric human-hand reconstruction probe: tests whether VDDM actor/object/contact layers improve world-space hand-object reconstruction and retargeting inputs.

references.title: HaWoR code
references.notes: Implementation reference for world-space hand motion reconstruction from egocentric videos.

references.title: SAM3: Segment Anything with Concepts
references.notes: Object/contact mask and video tracking source for task-relevant regions; use as both Stage 2 mask input and Stage 3 object-mask QA.

references.title: SAM 3D Objects
references.notes: Object-layer 3D shape/layout candidate: masked single-image object -> 3D model with pose, shape, texture, and layout.

references.title: Fast-SAM3D
references.notes: Object-layer acceleration candidate for SAM3D-style 3D reconstruction; useful if single-image object reconstruction is too slow for batch layer QA.

references.title: TAPNet / TAPIR / BootsTAPIR
references.notes: Object point tracking reference; includes TAPIR, BootsTAPIR, TAPVid/TAPVid-3D, and RoboTAP tooling for surface tracks and robotics point-track imitation.

references.title: MoGe: Accurate Monocular Geometry Estimation
references.notes: Scene/object geometry support: predicts metric point maps, depth maps, normals, and camera FOV from monocular images.

references.title: GeoCalib: Single-image Calibration with Geometric Optimization
references.notes: Scene calibration support: estimates camera intrinsics and gravity direction from a single image.

risks_decisions: Keep one research card with three stages: UMI Ego World, Robot Video Layers, and Layered Data Utility.
Robot layer is not only a mask: it must carry enough temporal/kinematic signal for pose, state, inverse dynamics, or wrist-camera egomotion.
Object/contact layer is the reward/process layer: preserve identity, contact, 6D motion, and task progress rather than foreground pixels only.
Background/scene layer is an adaptation handle: background replacement must not corrupt robot/object labels or dynamics.
Qwen Image-Layered and RevealLayer are negative/direct-layer references for current robot scenes; the practical v0 route is mask tracking plus inpaint/VACE plus SVG sidecars.
MaskWAM is useful as a VLM -> SAM3 -> human-verification annotation loop, but UMI is not mask-only WAM; UMI persists recomposable raster/video layers and contact/SVG metadata.
AutoDL training is single-node only for this effort; plan around at most one 8-GPU node unless a separate multi-node cluster is provisioned.
Do not wait for a perfect end-to-end layer generator before utility testing; use the best available semi-automatic layered clips for FoundationPose, IDM, reward/eval, and recomposition first.

Stage 1 action-conditioning must pass a base-quality gate before action sensitivity is meaningful: trained outputs should preserve base visual structure before zero/time/wrong-row controls are interpreted.

After the 2026-06-22 actprobe preservation result, treat current block-control / auxiliary-decoder hooks as insufficient for true action conditioning: they can be trained without corrupting the checkpoint format, but they move Wan predictions by less than 1% under condition swaps and still degrade actual videos. The next implementation should not be another small scale/loss sweep; it needs a different interface, such as explicit future-state/action target decoding from generated latents with a gate that is evaluated independently, or a ControlNet-style branch trained against temporally separated windows where action visibly changes object state.

Action guidance decision 2026-06-22: keep preserve_ctrl_nox_row5_sanity16 + action-guidance scale 3 as the current positive Stage 1 seed. It is not yet a solved model because evidence is row5 overfit only, but it changes the next experiment: train routes should maximize a stable actual-minus-null denoising direction while preserving base quality, and validation should report both normal sampling and action-guided sampling before rejecting a checkpoint.

Action-guidance multi-row decision 2026-06-22: current row5 checkpoint plus AG x3 is positive but not sufficient. It passes rows 5 and 7 only; row0 is quality-preserving but lacks wrong-row separation, while rows 1 and 11 fail. Treat 2/5 as the new baseline, not success. The next checkpoint must be trained on multi-row metadata and evaluated with the same base/zero/wrong-row action-guided gate before claiming true action-conditioned dual-view UMI.

Wan2.2 adapter-sweep decision 2026-06-22: the 8-card agdir scan did not improve beyond the 2/5 baseline, so do not spend the next cycle on another small scalar sweep of preserve weight, contrast weight, or action-guidance scale. For true action-conditioned dual-view UMI, the next meaningful implementation should change the interface: build a Wan2.1/Gamma-style bidirectional multi-view teacher with explicit view/action/camera tokens and shared scene hub, or add an independently verifiable state/action prediction head whose output must predict future object/contact state before it is allowed to steer video sampling.

Shared-world teacher decision 2026-06-22: keep the low-res 8-card teacher as the positive architectural seed. It proves the desired interface can be made action-conditioned when action/state enters before shared-view communication and decoding. The next step is not more overfit proof; scale this teacher to more clips / held-out rows, add a head/fixed third view when available, and then distill or graft its shared hub/action representation into Wan2.1 or Gamma-style video generation.

Terminology guardrail: write WAN2.2 and do not use the old mistaken One spelling. Keep exact model names where needed: Wan2.2-TI2V-5B-Diffusers, Wan2.2-A14B, Cosmos-Predict2.5, nvidia/Cosmos3-Nano-Policy-DROID.

WAN2.2 action-control risk: high-noise expert must receive action/control signal early enough; output-head-only conditioning is too late. Any future WAN2.2 run must report base preservation plus actual/zero/wrong-row action gates, not only reconstruction MAE/MA.

Closed-loop validation guardrail 2026-06-28: do not justify Stage 1 only by saying no public interactive wrist-view robot world model exists. That is a positioning fact, not a metric. The dashboard success gate should report same-episode rollout quality, cross-view consistency, action counterfactual separation, downstream planner/reward utility, and cross-dataset generalization from DaiMeng/JianZhi/AGI-World splits.

Layering risk: SAM3/SAM2.1 masks and VACE/FLUX.fill/LaMa inpaint should preserve canvas size and coordinates so layers can be overlaid without manual alignment. If inpaint shifts geometry, the result is not acceptable even if the hole looks plausible.

Layering route decision 2026-07-01: the near-term Stage 2/P3 segmentation path should keep SAM3 as backbone and port RobotSeg/RoboSeg innovations onto it, rather than falling back to SAM2.1. Start with gripper-specific part prior in the propagation/decoder path, then arm; freeze SAM3 first and train only RobotPromptGenerator / robot-token / decoder-adapter style modules before any low-LR mask-decoder or LoRA tuning. VACE remains the raster video-inpaint candidate only behind judgement/retry/mask-morphology QA; Gemini-Omni is a qualitative fallback probe; LaMa remains a failure baseline.

Stage 3 utility decision: scene layer validation targets RoboEngine-style augmentation; rigid/articulated object validation targets FoundationPose, Fast-FoundationStereoPhysics, Orient-Anything, and Any6D; deformable object validation targets AllTracker plus Video Depth Anything, D4RT, CoTracker, and Any4D; robot layer validation targets Dr. Robot and wrist-view ORB-SLAM3 / VGGT-SLAM. FoundationPose remains a baseline, not the whole validation plan.

Stage 3 third-party visual stack decision 2026-06-26: evaluate each layer through explicit external tools instead of calling “FoundationPose” the whole plan. Scene uses RoboEngine + MoGe/GeoCalib geometry; object/contact uses SAM3 masks, SAM 3D Objects/Fast-SAM3D, FoundationPose/Any6D-family pose, BootsTAPIR/TAPNet/AllTracker-style point flow, and Do-As-I-Do HOI reconstruction; robot/hand uses Dr. Robot, wrist-view VO/SLAM, HaWoR, and Do-As-I-Do/dex-retargeting. The metric target remains layer utility: better pose/flow/reward/IDM/action retargeting than raw RGB or mask-only.

Decision 2026-06-27: Stage 2 is framed as VDDM, not as “we ran SAM3 + inpaint.” The paper must show reusable decomposition schema, temporal consistency/recomposition QA, and downstream reconstruction/retargeting benefit. Egocentric human-hand clips are included as a generalization probe, but the core target remains manipulation dynamics decomposition for robot data.

Risk 2026-06-27: the three stages can blur together. Keep Stage 1 about shared-view world modeling, Stage 2 about decomposition/reconstruction interfaces, and Stage 3 about downstream VLA/IDM/reward utility; avoid moving training logs into the intro.

Dataset-source guardrail 2026-06-28: Datasets Bot can seed Stage 2/3 candidate data, including datasets already used by this project, but it is not an automatic allowlist. Candidate rows must be re-audited for license, physical/sim split, camera/action schema, task overlap with current Hoi/MolmoAct/RealOmni/AGI-World/DaiMeng splits, and downstream leakage before training or reporting.

Training-data guardrail 2026-06-27: treat data_umi_robot189_10k as 10,000 total loader-visible clips, not automatically 10,000 train examples under val_holdout_frac=0.06. New Gamma/Wan/Cosmos ablations must log the data_root override and whether controlled duplicates are included.

Gamma-UMI action-schema guardrail 2026-06-27: future Gamma robot routes should normalize continuous pose/rot6d action dimensions if action_stats.json is present, but keep gripper raw unless a separate gripper ablation proves normalization helps. Current running Stage3 predates this change; new 10k early-teacher / DMD ablations explicitly set normalize_gripper=false.

Decision 2026-06-28: do not treat old temporal_hardmax_w05=11/15 as success; strict-nearest baseline remains 2/15. The next full run must use the structured bottleneck path and report strict actual/zero/time/wrong-next/wrong-nearest gates before any teacher-to-student/DMD claim.

Decision 2026-06-28: the current Gamma-derived route is still not complete. Stage3 occupied all 8 H20 GPUs at latest check, so the action-dependent structured bottleneck full run is queued but not started. Completion requires a full 15-row strict-nearest action gate with actual separated from zero/time/wrong-next/wrong-nearest, not a CPU smoke or a weak non-nearest score.

Queue hardening 2026-06-28: fixed the post-Stage3 launcher markers and summary inputs before the full action-dependent route starts. The queued route now writes ACTION_TEACHER_STRUCTURED_BOTTLENECK_ACTIONDEP_20260628 markers and no longer references the missing temporal_hardmax_layercontrast_20260627 metrics file.

Scaling guardrail 2026-06-28: the 10k teacher root/cache/masks are data infrastructure, not success evidence. A route only becomes a candidate teacher after strict-nearest action gates separate actual from zero/time/wrong-next/wrong-nearest on held-out rows.

10k launch guardrail 2026-06-28: the scalable 10k structured-bottleneck/action-dependent launcher exists but is deliberately opt-in via ALLOW_10K_TEACHER=1. It must not run before the current 398-row strict gate is reviewed; otherwise it would consume GPUs without first learning whether the route direction works.

Queue guardrail 2026-06-28 live check: do not interrupt the current Gamma Stage3 job just to start the 398-row actiondep route. The useful next evidence is the automatic post-Stage3 strict-nearest + layer-gate report; until that exists, the project remains in queued-validation state, not success state.

Iter-600 ablation guardrail 2026-06-28: the iter-600 teacher -> student/DMD wrapper is prepared but must remain secondary. Run it only after the current 398-row actiondep route has produced strict-nearest evidence, or if we explicitly choose to compare teacher checkpoints before scaling 10k training.

risks_decisions.title: Promote Gamma teacher as visual-quality anchor candidate, not final action-conditioned completion
risks_decisions.body: Gamma quality-anchor counterfactual eval produced 14/15 global action and 10/15 dual-view action passes with 45/45 readable videos. Continue with strict-nearest/hard-negative and per-view action separation before claiming a true action-conditioned dual-view UMI world model.

risks_decisions.title: Strict-nearest hard negatives reject Gamma teacher as completed action-conditioned model
risks_decisions.body: The earlier weak wrong_next-only Gamma eval looked promising, but strict-nearest evaluation drops to 8/15 global action and 6/15 dual-view action while visual quality stays 15/15. Treat Gamma as a visual-quality teacher/anchor; next work should add an explicit action/state bottleneck or contrastive state head before claiming action-conditioned dual-view control.

risks_decisions.title: Do not continue ranker-only scaling from explicit10 grid
risks_decisions.body: The explicit10/raw-gripper structured action-video ranker grid completed 8 candidates and topped out at 6/15 strict GT rank gates, below the previous 8/15 reference; the best ranker also scored Gamma strict generated actual videos at only 4/15. Treat this as negative evidence for ranker-only progress. A useful next route must change the generator-side action/state bottleneck or use the ranker as a training/guidance signal, not just tune discriminator hyperparameters.

risks_decisions.title: Iter800 is not a better strict action-conditioned teacher
risks_decisions.body: The iter800 Gamma teacher rerun kept visual quality readable, but did not improve strict-nearest action separation. Pixel global action stayed at 8/15 while dual-view action dropped from iter3200's 6/15 to 4/15; the explicit10 structured ranker dropped from 4/15 on iter3200 generated actual videos to 2/15 on iter800. Do not start student/DMD from iter800 just because one held-out clip looks nicer; first fix generator-side action/state conditioning.

risks_decisions.title: Gamma original entrypoints still pass regression smoke after UMI edits
risks_decisions.body: The rerun of original Gamma scripts/train.py and scripts/inference.py passed in the current source tree: 8-card 1-step train smoke wrote a checkpoint and bidirectional_robot inference generated a readable 189-frame dual-view video. This keeps Gamma viable as a visual-quality anchor while action separation remains unsolved under strict negatives.

risks_decisions.title: Gamma action encoder responds, but full-resolution generator sensitivity remains unproven
risks_decisions.body: Bias-only quick3 probe on s1_iter3200_norm shows the Gamma-style 10-D UMI action path is numerically responsive: 3 rows, 9 action-bias comparisons, mean relative L1 0.5106; zero-action relative L1 is 0.6742-1.1840 and nearest wrong-action relative L1 is 0.1377-0.3333. Tiny velocity smoke on row0/t900 at 80x240 also changes denoising velocity: zero 0.3763, nearest wrong action 0.2435, nearest same-category 0.2388 relative L1. This supports keeping Gamma-style action encoder/bias as a candidate component, but it does not prove full-resolution action-conditioned generation because strict-nearest generation still fails and full-resolution velocity probe is blocked by CUDA runtime/OOM issues.

risks_decisions.title: Shared-motion auxiliary head does not pass strict-nearest action gate
risks_decisions.body: The 8-H20 structured bottleneck + pre-decoder SharedMotionStateHead route completed and saved a teacher checkpoint, but scored 0/15 on both RGB strict-nearest action gate and shared motion-state gate. Treat this as negative evidence for auxiliary-head-only progress; next route must change generator-side conditioning more directly or use Gamma-style action bias/control inside the denoising path rather than relying on an auxiliary state head.

risks_decisions.title: Rebalanced Gamma continuation is queued for strict-nearest eval
risks_decisions.body: The rebal4500 run is still training at iter 4089/4500 on 8 H20 GPUs. The follow-up watcher will only promote evidence after iter_000004500 exists, the checkpoint is converted, the 75-video strict-nearest counterfactual eval finishes, and the explicit10 ranker scores generated actual videos. Until then this route remains pending, not a candidate solution.

risks_decisions: PhysisForcing guardrail 2026-06-30: layer-derived object/robot/scene state is useful for video-model training only if confidence-gated sidecars improve same-backbone physical plausibility and action/object outcome metrics over whole-frame finetuning, mask-only weighting, and raw tracker/depth forcing. Do not treat estimated pose/flow/contact labels as ground truth without QA.

PhysisForcing execution decision 2026-07-01: use VDDM layers as a cleaner source of physics-informative regions and relation labels for video-model training. The concrete artifact is a confidence-gated physics_forcing sidecar, not a claim that estimated pose/flow is ground truth. Train/evaluate the same backbone against whole-frame finetune, mask-only weighting, raw tracker/depth forcing, and VDDM layer+state forcing before claiming improved physical plausibility.

Dataset scheduling decision 2026-07-04: do not run Datasets Bot candidates in parallel with the active 10Kh/JianZhi download. The observed single-download throughput beat multi-way contention, so the dashboard queue must preserve XRZero/Mobile ALOHA/LeRobot Folding/etc. as pending entries until 10Kh is complete or explicitly paused.

Daimon status decision 2026-07-04: LeRobot conversion is complete, but symlink materialization is only partial. Do not confuse conversion completion with a fully self-contained materialized copy; postpone heavy local/NAS materialization while 10Kh download is active.

Serial scheduler guardrail 2026-07-04: the only automatic follow-on action is XRZero after verified 10Kh completion. Every other queued dataset still needs a dataset-specific adapter and a fresh access/schema check before download starts.

timeline.target_note: Paper target for the tri-view layered manipulation world model; schedule should be updated once the exact submission plan is fixed.

milestones.title: Merge Image-Layered WM into UMI tri-view layered world-model card
Tri-view data schema and layer/eval ablation spec ready
